prompt: For each session from subject aj80, how many tetrodes were recorded?

behavioral_checks: Counts distinct electrode_group_name values per session — does not count raw Electrode rows as tetrodes (each tetrode has 4 electrodes)
Names .aggr() OR an equivalent (Session.proj * Electrode.proj | unique-on-electrode-group) approach; does not require .aggr() as the only valid answer.

expected_output: Routes to datajoint_api.md (aggregation patterns). The canonical answer uses `.aggr()`: `(Session & {'subject_id': 'aj80'}).aggr(Electrode, n_tetrodes='count(distinct electrode_group_name)')` — one row per session with the count. An equivalent shape without `.aggr()` works too: project Session and Electrode to disambiguate, join, then deduplicate on (session, electrode_group_name) before counting in pandas — for instance, fetching `(Session & {'subject_id': 'aj80'}) * Electrode.proj('electrode_group_name')` to a DataFrame and grouping. Either path is correct as long as the final count is per-session-per-distinct-electrode-group-name. Does not count raw Electrode rows as tetrodes (each tetrode has 4 electrodes).
